name: Snowflake
description: Cloud data platform for data warehousing and analytics. Monitor database access and user accounts.

config.setupInstructions:
1. Find your Account Identifier from your Snowflake URL:
   https://<account_identifier>.snowflakecomputing.com
   Example: 'myorg-myaccount'

2. Set up a Network Policy:
   Snowflake requires a network policy to use Programmatic Access Tokens.
   In your Snowflake account, create a network policy that includes CompAI's IP address: 54.159.77.69
   Then assign that network policy to the user who will generate the token.

3. Generate a Programmatic Access Token (PAT):
   Option A - In Snowsight, go to Admin > Users & Roles > select your user > Generate Token
   Option B - Use SQL to create a token for your user

   Note: The user's authentication policy must allow PATs. If you're having trouble generating a token, check your authentication policy settings.

4. Copy the token and paste both values below.

credentialFields.label: Account Identifier
credentialFields.helpText: Your Snowflake account identifier from your URL: https://<account_identifier>.snowflakecomputing.com (e.g. 'myorg-myaccount').

credentialFields.label: Programmatic Access Token (PAT)
credentialFields.helpText: Generate via Snowsight: Admin → Users → select user → Generate Token. Or SQL: ALTER USER <user> ADD PROGRAMMATIC ACCESS TOKEN <name> DAYS_TO_EXPIRY = 30.

checks.name: Snowflake App Availability
checks.description: Checks that the Snowflake API is accessible by listing databases.

checks.name: Snowflake Employee Access
checks.description: Verifies employee access by listing Snowflake user accounts.
